commands.author: jason-allen-oneal
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a87946c7d75c84c564f17fcd11b11c51c8fa831b)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a Local Gateway settings card for OpenClaw Onboard, a ShowGatewayWizard app command, five locale strings, and a tray contract-test assertion for direct wizard navigation.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR findings: CI reproduces the localization failure, and source inspection shows the direct-wizard command navigates an existing setup window while ProgressPage cancels on unload. I did not run the Windows UI locally in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the PR still needs contributor runtime proof and a product/UX call after the code blockers are fixed; ClawSweeper should not repair while proof is missing.

target.title: security: add Security Matrix runtime-fact audit model
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a Security Matrix runtime-fact evaluator.
- Models actor, influence source, tool capability, approval state, and operator policy as separate inputs.
- Adds default policy data, a deterministic evaluator, tool capability resolution, an audit-event object builder, tests, and documentation.
- Adds a before-tool-call fact adapter and anchors it to the trusted tool policy boundary through an inert audit-event builder.
- Removes the unsupported `security.matrix.audit.enabled` placeholder so this PR does not reserve a parallel config surface.
- Removes the Security Matrix production files from the required dead-code allowlist; they are now in the optional allowlist since the model is not yet wired into the runtime import graph.
- Hardens custom policy parsing so malformed decisions cannot weaken the default policy.

## Scope

This PR remains non-enforcing and observe-only.

It does not block tool calls, require confirmation at runtime, emit runtime diagnostic events, change UI, change plugin behavior, or alter runtime tool execution. The before-tool-call adapter only builds an audit event object from explicit boundary facts. Runtime diagnostic emission, canonical config, CLI display, and enforcement remain future work.

No default trusted `security_matrix.evaluated` log records are emitted. The adapter is a pure function that builds the audit event shape; no runtime diagnostic wiring is included.

## Changes since last review

- Removed Security Matrix production files from the **required** `KNIP_UNUSED_FILE_ALLOWLIST` (which would fail as stale once the files are reachable). They are now in `KNIP_OPTIONAL_UNUSED_FILE_ALLOWLIST` since the model is not yet wired into the production import graph.
- Clarified docs: the before-tool-call adapter defaults `approvalState` to `not_required`, while the low-level evaluator normalizes missing/undefined to `none`.
- No runtime diagnostic emission was added. The `trusted-tool-policy.ts` path does not import or call the Security Matrix adapter.

## Validation

Latest head: `b8f3e69aa3`.

Focused commands run against this head:

\`\`\`bash
pnpm test src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts
pnpm changed:lanes --base upstream/main --json
pnpm exec oxlint src/security/security-matrix/ src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/security/security-matrix.md
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
\`\`\`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Security Matrix runtime-fact evaluation produces deterministic audit decisions from actor, influence source, capability, approval state, and operator policy. The before-tool-call adapter builds the same audit-event shape from explicit boundary facts without emitting, blocking, or mutating execution. Malformed custom policy decisions are ignored instead of being treated as policy output.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw development environment (Node.js 22, pnpm, Linux). The Security Matrix evaluator and before-tool-call adapter were exercised through the unit test suite and lint/typecheck passes.

Exact commands and output:

\`\`\`
$ CI=true pnpm test src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts
 RUN  v4.1.7
 ✓  unit-fast  src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts (27 tests) 13ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  27 passed (27)
   Duration  266ms

$ pnpm changed:lanes --base upstream/main --json
{
  "lanes": { "core": true, "coreTests": true, "extensions": false, ... }
}

$ pnpm exec oxlint src/security/security-matrix/ src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 25ms on 8 files with 208 rules using 8 threads.

$ node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/security/security-matrix.md
Docs formatting clean (663 files).

$ git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
(no whitespace errors)
\`\`\`

Observed result: All 27 Security Matrix unit tests pass, lint passes with 0 warnings/errors, docs formatting is clean, and no whitespace errors. The required `KNIP_UNUSED_FILE_ALLOWLIST` is empty; Security Matrix production files are in the optional allowlist. No runtime diagnostic emission was added.

What was not tested: Runtime hook emission, runtime enforcement, strict config schema adoption, CLI display, plugin UI, and live tool execution are intentionally not changed by this PR.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
